  "findings": [
    "Worn sheave grooves causing the belt to ride too low in the groove (bottoming out on the inside diameter)."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Replace the worn sheave",
    "Inspect and replace the V-belt if damaged",
    "Re-align the sheaves and tension the new belt to manufacturer specifications"
  ],
  "rationale": "A belt riding low in the sheave groove indicates severe groove wear. When a V-belt bottoms out on the inside diameter of the groove, it loses its wedging action, leading to slippage, rapid belt wear, loss of power transmission, and potential heat damage. The sheave must be replaced before the asset is returned to service.",
  "references": [
    "mfr guides"
  ]
}
